description:
You are curating the configuration of a small, file-based artifact manager that lives in the directory
/home/user/artifactmgr.  
Two configuration files already exist:

1. /home/user/artifactmgr/repos.yaml – lists every binary repository in strict YAML 1.2 format.
2. /home/user/artifactmgr/settings.toml – holds per-repository feature flags in TOML.

Your job is to add support for a brand-new Maven repository called “edge-utils”.  
When you are finished, the system must look exactly like this:

--------------------------------------------------------------------
/home/user/artifactmgr/repos.yaml   (YAML 1.2, 2-space indentation)

repositories:
  - name: core-libs
    url: https://artifacts.example.com/core-libs
    type: maven
  - name: dev-tools
    url: https://artifacts.example.com/dev-tools
    type: npm
  - name: graphics
    url: https://artifacts.example.com/graphics
    type: docker
  - name: edge-utils
    url: https://artifacts.example.com/edge-utils
    type: maven
--------------------------------------------------------------------
/home/user/artifactmgr/settings.toml   (TOML v1.0.0)

[repository."core-libs"]
autopublish = true

[repository."dev-tools"]
autopublish = false

[repository."graphics"]
autopublish = true

[repository."edge-utils"]
autopublish = true
--------------------------------------------------------------------
/home/user/artifactmgr/update.log   (plain text, **one single line**, no extra spaces or newline at the end)

2024-01-18T00:00:00Z edge-utils repository added to repos.yaml and settings.toml by automation
--------------------------------------------------------------------

Important requirements that the automated checker will look for:  
• The YAML file must keep its original ordering, style, and 2-space indentation; simply append the new mapping for edge-utils as shown.  
• The TOML file must add the new [[repository]] table exactly as shown, including the quoting of "edge-utils" and the blank lines that separate the blocks.  
• The log file must be created if it does not yet exist, must contain **exactly** the single line shown above, and must NOT end with an extra newline character.  
• Perform all edits from the shell using any single compound command you like (pipe/redirect/&& is fine).  
• You may assume that /home/user/artifactmgr already exists and is writable by your user. No super-user privileges are available.

Once those three files match the exact byte-for-byte content above, the task is complete.
